# AI Description

# Technical Description of Figure A-3.1.1.6.(1)

## 1. Diagram Type
This is a **detail drawing/section diagram** illustrating the measurement methodology for fenestration and door areas in building envelope calculations.

## 2. Building Components Shown
The diagram depicts a cross-sectional view of a window installation showing:
- Wall framing assembly
- Window frame
- Window glazing unit
- Rough opening in wall

## 3. Visible Labels, Measurements, and Annotations

**Labels:**
- "wall framing" (pointing to the structural wall assembly)
- "window frame" (indicating the frame component)
- "window" (identifying the glazed area)
- "rough opening" (indicating the wall opening dimension)

**Reference Code:**
- EG00732B (diagram identification number in bottom right)

**Hatching/Patterns:**
- Diagonal hatching indicates the window frame components
- Cross-hatching indicates wall framing members

## 4. Relationships and Connections Between Elements

The diagram illustrates the dimensional relationship between:
- The **rough opening** (outer measurement) - shown with vertical arrows extending the full height of the opening in the wall
- The **window** (actual glazed unit) - shown with vertical arrows indicating the visible glass area
- The **window frame** - positioned between the rough opening and the window glazing
- The **wall framing** - surrounding the entire assembly

The vertical arrows clearly demonstrate that the rough opening dimension is larger than the actual window dimension, with the window frame occupying the intermediate space.

## 5. Compliance Requirements and Technical Specifications

**Per Section A-3.1.1.6.(1):**

This diagram supports the Code's methodology for calculating fenestration areas, which differs from CSA A440.2/A440.3 standards. 

**Key Compliance Point:**
- The NECB uses **rough opening dimensions** (not actual window dimensions) for calculating fenestration area
- This approach facilitates determination of building code compliance
- The rough opening measurement includes the window frame and the actual glazed area

**Referenced Standards:**
- Contrasts with CSA A440.2-14 "Fenestration Energy Performance/User Guide to CSA A440.2-14, Fenestration Energy Performance" methodology
- Applies to both windows and doors as stated in Sentence 3.1.1.6.(1)

**Purpose:**
This measurement approach simplifies compliance verification by using the dimensions of rough openings rather than requiring precise measurements of installed fenestration products.
